description:
You are a log-analyst who has just received an application event file stored at  

/home/user/logs/app.log  

The file already exists.  It contains exactly five (5) single-line JSON objects, one per line.

Your job is to

1. Validate every log-line against the following JSON “schema” rules:  
   • The keys timestamp (string), level (string) and message (string) must be present.  
   • The value of level must be one of the literals INFO, WARN or ERROR (case-sensitive).  
   • When level == "ERROR", an additional key error_code (string) MUST be present; for all other levels this key is OPTIONAL.  
   • No further structural requirements apply.  

2. Produce two artefacts, both inside the directory /home/user/output/ (the directory already exists but is empty):  

   a) A validation report named /home/user/output/validation_report.log  
      • One line of text per original log-line, in the original order.  
      • The format must be:  
        Line <n>: VALID  
        or  
        Line <n>: INVALID - <short reason>  
      • <n> is the 1-based line number in app.log.  
      • The literal strings “VALID” and “INVALID” must be uppercase.  
      • There must be *exactly* one space after the colon.  
      • Use the reason text “missing error_code” when rule #3 is violated; use “schema error” for any other rule violation.  
      • Terminate every line with a Unix newline (LF).  

   b) A JSON summary file named /home/user/output/error_summary.json  
      • It must contain only ERROR-level events that passed validation.  
      • The required document structure is:

        {
          "summary_generated_at": "2023-10-01T00:00:00Z",
          "total_error_events": <number>,
          "error_counts": {
            "<error_code_1>": <count>,
            "<error_code_2>": <count>,
            ...
          }
        }

        – Use the literal timestamp string "2023-10-01T00:00:00Z" for summary_generated_at (do NOT compute the current time).  
        – The JSON must be minified exactly as shown above: each key/value pair on its own line, two spaces of indentation, and no trailing whitespace.  
        – The keys inside error_counts must appear in alphanumeric order; each value is the integer count of that code among validated ERROR lines.  
        – Terminate the very last line with a Unix newline (LF).

3. Do not alter /home/user/logs/app.log.  Write only to /home/user/output/.

The automated grader will verify:

• That /home/user/output/validation_report.log exists and matches the required text literally, byte-for-byte.  
• That /home/user/output/error_summary.json exists and matches the exact JSON structure and ordering described above.  

Hints (do NOT print exact commands here):  
• jq -e can exit non-zero when a filter fails, which is useful for validation.  
• awk or nl can help with line numbering.  
• Remember to create or truncate the target files before writing into them so the grader sees only your results.

The whole task can be completed with 3–4 concise shell commands plus redirections/pipes.  Keep terminal output informative but make sure the two required files are produced exactly as specified.
truth:

-----------------------------------------------------------------
{"timestamp":"2023-10-01T12:00:00Z","level":"INFO","message":"Service started"}
{"timestamp":"2023-10-01T12:05:00Z","level":"ERROR","message":"Failed to connect to DB","error_code":"ERR001"}
{"timestamp":"2023-10-01T12:06:00Z","level":"ERROR","message":"Null pointer exception","error_code":"ERR002"}
{"timestamp":"2023-10-01T12:07:00Z","level":"ERROR","message":"Invalid input data"}
{"timestamp":"2023-10-01T12:08:00Z","level":"WARN","message":"High memory usage"}
-----------------------------------------------------------------

Directory /home/user/output/ exists (mode 755) and is empty.

Expected artefacts AFTER correct task completion:

1. /home/user/output/validation_report.log  (mode 644, LF endings)
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Line 1: VALID
Line 2: VALID
Line 3: VALID
Line 4: INVALID - missing error_code
Line 5: VALID
-----------------------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/output/error_summary.json  (mode 644, LF endings)
-----------------------------------------------------------------
{
  "summary_generated_at": "2023-10-01T00:00:00Z",
  "total_error_events": 2,
  "error_counts": {
    "ERR001": 1,
    "ERR002": 1
  }
}
-----------------------------------------------------------------
